sourceMovie.title: Bad Teacher
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Bad Teacher (2011) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: School of Rock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bad Teacher clicked for you, School of Rock (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Re-Animator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bad Teacher clicked for you, Re-Animator (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bad Teacher?
faq.answer: Try School of Rock, Re-Animator, The School for Good and Evil—each shares DNA with Bad Teacher while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bad Teacher?
faq.answer: Bad Teacher (2011) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bad Teacher worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
